title: Nautilocalyx bullatus
taxonomy: Nautilocalyx bullatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Nautilocalyx, which is a group of plants primarily found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. Within the Boraginaceae family, Nautilocalyx bullatus is classified as a species of shrub or subshrub. The classification of Nautilocalyx bullatus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Nautilocalyx bullatus, also known as Bullate Nautilocalyx, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations.
